item.title: Xbox's Confusing Exclusivity Criteria Now Aided by 'EXCLUSIVE' Label on Console Dashboard
item.content:encoded:
Xbox has rolled out a new label for console exclusive games, visible now on your Xbox Series X/S dashboard.

This month, Xbox confirmed it had decided to make the upcoming Gears of War: E-Day a console exclusive when it arrives on October 6. Clockwork Revolution will also be a console exclusive when it launches in 2027.

Now, Gears of War: E-Day features a brand new "EXCLUSIVE" label, similar to other previous game labels that denote a game is optimized for Series X/S, or featured as part of a subscription service such as Game Pass or Ubisoft+.

The label certainly makes clear the game will no longer be arriving for PlayStation 5 (as had once been planned), though it's worth remembering that the title will still also launch on PC.

Still, the label will likely prove useful helping mark out the titles that Microsoft has decided it will make exclusive — something chief content officer Matt Booty has said will be chosen on a "case-by-case basis", but not include "big multiplayer games and live-service games," which will remain multiplatform.

There's no sign yet of a similar label on the game's Xbox.com store page (or the one for Clockwork Revolution), but with its launch still not for several months, there's still time.

"We want a reason for people to get on board with Xbox, we want them to have a reason to buy an Xbox, we want them to have a reason to be an Xbox fan," Booty said previously. "At the same time, we want to reward all our players that have been with us for a long time — we know that exclusives are important, and that's why we've got Gears coming in 2026 and Clockwork [Revolution] coming in 2027."

"We also want to be clear that our big multiplayer games and live-service games are going to continue to be multiplatform," he continued. "If we've promised something to players already, we're going to honor that promise. And then — I think Asha said it — we're going to make the right decision and not the fast decision.

The 100 Best Xbox Games of All Time

"We're going to keep thinking about this going forward," Booty concluded, "and, I think you guys know already, our principle is when we announce the date, we announce the platforms. So, it's going to be case-by-case, but we're going to be clear, that when it's got a date, it's got a platform and you'll know what the choice is going to be."

This approach has been dubbed as confusing by some fans, and feels like it will take a while to settle down. As newly-installed Xbox CEO Asha Sharma has said, the company will firstly honor all previously-announced games coming to PlayStation (including Halo: Campaign Evolved, for example) and look to make more unannounced games exclusive if and when the company's finances improve.

In other words, for now, expect more announcements of new Xbox titles where PlayStation 5 and even Nintendo Switch 2 versions are confirmed as well. This was the case just this month, when Xbox made State of Decay 3's PlayStation 5 version made official, Hellblade threequel Senua was unveiled and confirmed for PS5, and Spyro: A Realm Beyond was detailed as coming to Xbox, PS5 and Nintendo Switch 2.

Meanwhile, it's been reported that a new PlayStation business document has highlighted an official change to Sony's own release strategy, with PC no longer described as part of the company's first-party launch focus.

Tom Phillips is IGN's News Editor. You can reach Tom at tom_phillips@ign.com or find him on Bluesky @tomphillipseg.bsky.social

Recommends

In This Article

item.title: PUBG has genAI team mates now capable of "intelligent decision-making", and I must remind you that Krafton are working on bots for the military
item.content:encoded:
Winner winner, Terminator

PUBG: Battlegrounds – aka Plunkbat – is getting generative AI team mates in the shape of PUBG Ally Duo, which is available now in beta. The new functionality uses Nvidia tech to simulate "autonomous game characters that use AI to perceive, plan, and take action within their environment". They also have speech models so that they can do bantz.

It all reflects publisher Krafton's wider investments in generative AI, which have seen them team up with an actual military company to create unpiloted robot weapon systems - a project that will draw on Krafton's expertise in "operating large-scale game data and physics based virtual worlds". So you know, do consider that when you enlist a Plunkbot to acquire some chicken dinner, you're also possibly training Ultron.

Here is a gushing marketing summary of how PUBG Ally works, snipped from Nvidia's website.

To balance split-second combat reflexes with intelligent decision-making, PUBG Ally divides its workload into two interconnected systems. The first is a traditional, fast-paced behavior tree that handles immediate tactical actions such as movement, precise aiming, and instant combat responses. The second is NVIDIA ACE, which drives the teammate's cognitive abilities using AI models and tools. Defining the boundary between these two systems required extensive iteration, as perfecting the division was essential to keeping PUBG Ally responsive enough for high-stakes combat while preserving the flexible, natural feel of a true teammate.

This cognitive system leverages three distinct models running locally on RTX GPUs with at least 8GB of VRAM. First, a lightweight NVIDIA Parakeet speech-to-text model precisely interprets English voice commands in real-time. Next, NVIDIA Mistral-Nemo-Minitron, a 2 billion parameter small language model, analyzes the situation and generates context-aware responses in English. Finally, a custom KRAFTON in-house text-to-speech model synthesizes natural-sounding voice responses instantly. To maximize performance and keep latency to a minimum, an in-game inferencing toolset streamlines AI execution directly alongside the game's graphics engine.

The Plunkbot beta will run until 30th June, via Ally Duo Mode in PUBG Arcade. The Nvidia post stresses that the beta will allow Krafton "to collect invaluable real-world player feedback, to guide the future of AI agents in games".

I'm writing this up mostly because as one of the founding battle royales, Plunkbat defines a particular kind of team chemistry over voice chat that has fuelled a lot of Youtube careers. Strange to think of LLMs and SSMs in that context.

It makes me wonder if we're on the brink of another age in Let's Play culture, whereby the more charismatic genAI bots emerge as co-stars. Urgh, I'm not especially plugged into the world of streaming, but I can sort of see this. PUBG's rival Fortnite already has some spontaneously sweary Darth Vader functionality, with plans to let players create their own haunted chatbot NPCs.

Ah, I miss the pre-recorded bot slagging matches of Quake 3. Anyway, here is a more considered, interview-led piece from Samuel Horti about the growing prevalence of LLM-voiced NPCs.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/pubg-has-genai-team-mates-now-capable-of-intelligent-decision-making-and-i-must-remind-you-that-krafton-are-working-on-bots-for-the-military

item.title: Onimusha: Way of the Sword Release Date Might Come Sooner
item.content:encoded:
It seems that Onimusha: Way of the Sword’s developers are moving up the title’s release date, with several retailers listing a new September release date.

If you caught any of the recent announcements during the latest Summer Game Fest, you likely noticed that many games are now aiming for a September release window. This decision has been made due to one reason: GTA 6 is coming out in November. Now, Capcom has apparently moved up the release date for Onimusha to avoid competing with the crowd.

Before we get into the full story, consider downloading the free Insider Gaming app for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

Onimusha: Way of the Sword’s New Release Date Set for September 4

Spotted by Cheap Ass Gamer on BlueSky, several retailers have listed a new release date for Onimusha: Way of the Sword, including PNP Games, Best Buy, and more. The initial release date was set for September 25, and it has now been moved up to September 4 in these listings, creating space between games coming later that month. However, there have been no official announcements from Capcom or Onimusha: Way of the Sword’s accounts.

If this is true, it makes a lot of sense, especially considering that many games are scheduled to release within the same timeframe. To provide some context, several highly anticipated games have chosen September as their launch month. The month kicks off with The Blood of Dawnwalker on September 3, which is very close to the new release date for Onimusha. Next, we have Marvel’s Wolverine arriving on September 15.

More Insider Gaming News: Sony Isn’t Getting ‘Revenge’ on Bungie For Destiny Underperforming, It’s Claimed

At the end of the month, coinciding with Onimusha’s original release date, two more major titles are set to debut: Control: Resonant and Silent Hill: Townfall, both releasing on September 24. September 2026 appears to be an exciting month for gamers, but it is not for developers and publishers, as they need their titles to stand out against such heavy competition.

What are your thoughts on Onimusha: Way of the Sword seemingly moving up the release date? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Sony clarifying PlayStation AI usage. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Two Point Museum x DAVE THE DIVER collaboration update now available
item.content:encoded:
You made it just in time, friends. Someone spilt coffee all over an arcade machine, which has only gone and caused a minor temporal distortion in spacetime. Now, strange rifts have opened, leading to other worlds in a place/not-place they’ve named The Digiverse. If you need a reminder of what the Digiverse is, you can read more about the digital phenomenon here. [OG blog link]

We’re thrilled to announce our next Digiverse Rift is in partnership with MINTROCKET and DAVE THE DIVER, where you’ll not only explore an ocean of discoveries, but this time, the temporal spill has gone both ways, and you’ll find Museum goodies in the latest DAVE THE DIVER downloadable content…

So you can curate the deep alongside historic displays, we’ve bundled up two deeply different management games with a catalogue of collectables. We’ve joint forces with DAVE THE DIVER with a 20 percent discounted Steam bundle.

The DAVE THE DIVER Rift is swimming into Two Point Museum right NOW, available for all platforms. So dive deep, curators!

DAVE THE DIVER

Welcome to the Blue Hole!

Hungry guests in your museum? As you’re used to jumping into newly developed Rifts in the very fabric of the universe, an ever-changing, vast, deep body of water is nothing, right?!

Grab some snorkels and dust off your harpoons, as you explore the shifting depths of the Blue Hole to discover a diverse menu of Marine Life.

Alongside new fish for your aquarium, you’ll bring back a few relics of the Sea People. Recreate their underwater village with aquarium decor that will have your new Marine Life exhibits feeling right at home.

​And what good is discovering an abundance of marine life if you can’t monetise it for hungry museum-goers?!

As museum managers, you can now establish your very own Bancho Sushi, building the Sushi Bar to satiate guests with fresh fish. By serving an edible exhibit, you’ll turn Marine Life exhibits into sushi. Guests served in the Sushi Bar receive Buzz and go on to make a special donation.

Plus, you can decorate your museum with new Sushi Bar decor items, including a Bancho Lantern, a new potted plant, and more!

Whilst exploring the depths of the Blue Hole, things can get preeeetty dangerous; you might want some spare oxygen for those deeper dives. Oxygen canisters are now available as Cargo Items! Built in the Workshop, these items will equip your experts with all they’ll need to counter (some) of the perils they may encounter.

So, what’s new?

  • Experts can now explore the world of DAVE THE DIVER, visiting three Points of Interest in the Blue Hole.
  • Collect a diverse menu of Marine Life, with six new exhibits.
  • Create a vibe fit to suit the Sea People with new aquarium decorative items.
  • Open a brand new business, building your very own Bancho Sushi Bar in your museum and serve fresh fish.

​As usual, this update also comes with a load of bug fixes, improvements and optimization changes.

Anniversary Cake and Outfit

It’s a special time for the studio, as very soon, we turn ten! To mark the occasion, there are new Anniversary Items in-game so all Pointians can join in with the celebrations.

​Don your Staff with two new anniversary outfits, the Cake Topper and Party Hat. Plus, you can even get your guests involved with the new Anniversary Cake, a self-serve cake display where guests and staff alike can share a slice to increase their Food need.

Choose an Exhibit

A few months ago, we released a blog all about how we take on and action feedback from you, lovely Pointians, and one of your top requests was the ability to set up repeating Expeditions. If you didn’t see that blog piece, we went into detail about how that was technically difficult, and we promised our design team were cooking up something to help.

Well, they’re here to DELIVER. Now, once a Point of Interest reaches max Survey Level, you can select which exhibit you want on the Expedition setup screen. Choosing an Exhibit increases the Expedition’s cost and duration; however, you can be guaranteed to get the exhibit you choose.

​Now, the pay-off here is that it doesn’t guarantee the Exhibit’s quality; you still need to use all the tools at your disposal to increase the chances of finding higher-quality Exhibits, but, for anyone looking to complete their Pristine collection, it’s now all the easier to guarantee the exhibit you want.

Twitch Drops

Celebrate our birthday in style, with some throwback items available as Twitch Drops. To earn Twitch Drops, watch your favorite streamers until 16th July to grab these drops celebrating our birthday.

The items can be earned by watching Drop-Enabled streamers play Two Point Museum—you’ll know if a streamer has drop-enabled as it displays in their tags on Twitch!

Watch 15 minutes to earn the Milk Keg, an item you might remember from your campus days. The Milk Keg is a wall-mounted unit that meets guests’ Drink need.

After 30 minutes, you unlock the Leaflet Info stand, reminiscent of the leaflets in Two Point Hospital. This item will inform your guests all about your wonderful exhibits. And last but not least, by watching for 45 minutes, you’ll unlock the Ticket Reception, a throwback to our roots in the hospital again. This works just like a normal ticket desk.

Two Point Museum Free to Play Weekend!

We’re excited to announce that Two Point Museum is FREE to play this weekend, starting 18:00 BST today you can jump in and try your hand at curation. If you’ve ever been on the fence about playing, now is the perfect time to try.

Build incredible museums, discover bizarre artefacts to exhibit, and curate the perfect museum visit for your guests. Play for free now on Steam and Xbox until June 22.

Watch a new trailer below.
